title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear two people talking about a friend's wedding.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you decided what to wear to Amy's wedding? || Woman: I bought a dress last month, but now I'm not sure. The invitation says the ceremony is outside. || Man: So it might be cold? || Woman: Exactly. [8]I'm going to take a smart jacket as well, because the weather can change quickly by the lake[/8]. I don't want to spend the day shivering. || Woman: That sounds sensible. It's always easier when you know what to expect before you make a final decision.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you decided what to wear to Amy's wedding?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I bought a dress last month, but now I'm not sure. The invitation says the ceremony is outside.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So it might be cold?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. I'm going to take a smart jacket as well, because the weather can change quickly by the lake. I don't want to spend the day shivering.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That sounds sensible. It's always easier when you know what to expect before you make a final decision.

questions.question: What is the woman worried about?

options.A: being too cold at the wedding
options.B: wearing clothes that are not smart enough

questions.explanation: She says she will take a jacket because the weather can change quickly by the lake.

extracts.context: You will hear a man telling a colleague about working from home.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: How are you finding working from home twice a week? || Man: It's useful. I get more done in the morning because nobody interrupts me. || Woman: Do you miss the office? || Man: Sometimes. [9]I find it harder to stop working at the end of the day because my laptop is still there on the kitchen table[/9]. I need a better routine. || Man: I see what you mean. Sometimes those small details matter more than people expect at first.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: How are you finding working from home twice a week?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It's useful. I get more done in the morning because nobody interrupts me.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Do you miss the office?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Sometimes. I find it harder to stop working at the end of the day because my laptop is still there on the kitchen table. I need a better routine.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. Sometimes those small details matter more than people expect at first.

questions.question: What problem does the man have when working from home?

options.B: He works for longer than he should.
options.C: He cannot use his laptop properly.

questions.explanation: He says it is harder to stop working at the end of the day.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a concert ticket.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Did you get tickets for the concert? || Man: Yes, but only one. I waited too long, and the cheaper seats had gone. || Woman: Oh no. Are you still going? || Man: I think so. [10]The seat is right at the side, so I probably won't see the whole stage, but I really want to hear the band live[/10]. || Woman: Well, I hope it works out, because it sounds as if you've thought about it carefully.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Did you get tickets for the concert?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, but only one. I waited too long, and the cheaper seats had gone.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Oh no. Are you still going?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I think so. The seat is right at the side, so I probably won't see the whole stage, but I really want to hear the band live.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Well, I hope it works out, because it sounds as if you've thought about it carefully.

questions.question: What is the disadvantage of the man's ticket?

options.A: It was more expensive than expected.
options.B: It is for the wrong date.
options.C: It may not give him a good view.

questions.explanation: He says the seat is at the side and he probably will not see the whole stage.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about a new library.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you visited the new library yet? || Woman: Yes, and it's excellent. The children's area is bright, and there are plenty of computers. || Man: Is it easy to find things? || Woman: [11]Much easier than before, because the books are organised by topic as well as by writer[/11]. I found what I needed quickly, which almost never happened in the old building. || Man: Anyway, let me know what happens, because I'm interested to hear how it goes next time.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you visited the new library yet?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, and it's excellent. The children's area is bright, and there are plenty of computers.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Is it easy to find things?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Much easier than before, because the books are organised by topic as well as by writer. I found what I needed quickly, which almost never happened in the old building.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Anyway, let me know what happens, because I'm interested to hear how it goes next time.

questions.question: What does the woman like about the new library?
questions.explanation: She says the books are organised better and she found what she needed quickly.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a language exchange.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Are you still going to the Spanish language exchange? || Man: Yes, though I was nervous at first. Everyone makes mistakes, so that's fine. || Woman: Do you speak Spanish all evening? || Man: Not really. [12]We spend half the time speaking English and half speaking Spanish, which makes it fair for everyone[/12]. That balance works well. || Woman: Yes, and at least you can plan better now, which should make things less stressful. || Man: That's true. It's much better to notice the problem early than be surprised later.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are you still going to the Spanish language exchange?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, though I was nervous at first. Everyone makes mistakes, so that's fine.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Do you speak Spanish all evening?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Not really. We spend half the time speaking English and half speaking Spanish, which makes it fair for everyone. That balance works well.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, and at least you can plan better now, which should make things less stressful.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's true. It's much better to notice the problem early than be surprised later.

questions.question: What does the man say about the language exchange?

options.A: People worry too much about mistakes.
options.B: The two languages are used equally.

questions.explanation: He says they spend half the time speaking English and half speaking Spanish.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking to a friend about a train journey.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: The journey was fine at first. The train left on time, and I had a reserved seat by the window. But after twenty minutes we stopped outside a station and waited there. [13]The driver explained there was a problem with the signals, so we arrived nearly forty minutes late[/13]. Luckily, my meeting had been moved to the afternoon. || Speaker: I told a friend about it later, and she said she had experienced something very similar.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: The journey was fine at first. The train left on time, and I had a reserved seat by the window. But after twenty minutes we stopped outside a station and waited there. The driver explained there was a problem with the signals, so we arrived nearly forty minutes late. Luckily, my meeting had been moved to the afternoon.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: I told a friend about it later, and she said she had experienced something very similar.

questions.question: Why was the woman's train delayed?

options.B: The train left the first station late.

questions.explanation: The woman says the driver explained there was a problem with the signals.
